summary: Heavily obfuscated index.js requires manual review
details: The package's index.js is heavily obfuscated with classic string-array shuffling patterns (e.g., while(!![]) loops with repeated ['shift']() calls and hex-encoded indices on a single line). Obfuscation of this shape is uncommon in legitimate library code and frequently used to hide install/runtime behavior, but no concrete installer-harm behavior (exfiltration endpoint, credential read, dropper, lifecycle hook) has been corroborated in this record. The package name 'bootstrap-utils' also has confusion potential with the popular 'bootstrap' ecosystem. A human reviewer should de-obfuscate index.js to confirm intent before this version is trusted.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
